audit: Route-D D4 Stueckelberg / anomalous-U(1) protection card

checks.name: premise: D3 pricing card all_pass with the d=5 escape closed
DYN-5 contamination ceilings imported and converted to absolute instanton actions S' = ln(1/eps): refreshed 7.72, loose 4.26
IDENTITY (single factor, exhaustive integer grid): Dirac + portal allowed => q(X L H) = q(X X) with ZERO violations
TWO abelian factors (exhaustive): the separation q(XLH) != q(XX) is impossible -- adding abelian factors can never help

B-L instance: the portal forces B-L(X) = -1 (ODD); then XX = X L Htilde = -2 while NN = +2 -- the 126bar matter parity (even-unit breaking) does NOT separate the X mass from the X-Dirac contamination, and the XX source is the CONJUGATE class of the D3 NN source
Lorentz fermion-parity gate: every SUSY-style m = 1 decorated channel has an ODD Weyl-fermion count and is forbidden outright in the non-SUSY component EFT (no operator of any dimension)
m = 2 decorations survive Lorentz but first arise at operator dimension 7 (four fermions + scalar): suppressed by (1/M_s)^3, negligible against every DYN-4 window
hence the ONLY renormalizable contamination channel in the non-SUSY EFT is the direct Dirac Yukawa X L Htilde -- exactly the channel the S1 no-go ties to the X mass insertion

the XX source must be LARGE (eps_XX = sqrt(3) M_*/M_s ~ 0.34 at M_s = 2e16, i.e. S_XX ~ 1.1): a generic same-charge spurion would put the X-Dirac coupling at 0.34 -- more than 2 orders above even the LOOSE ceiling; charge bookkeeping alone is dead (quantified form of the S1 no-go)

closure condition quantified: zero-mode selectivity must supply Delta S = S'(XLH) - S(XX) >= 6.6 (refreshed window) / 3.2 (loose) at M_s = 2e16; a DIRECT X L Htilde instanton needs S' >= 7.72 / 4.26 absolute

D3 consistency DISCLOSED: the NN tower source carries Delta(B-L) = +2 but the XX mass needs -2 -- the CONJUGATE instanton class; the shared D3+D4 bookkeeping therefore needs BOTH orientations present with independent actions (a doubled conditional assumption, priced -- NOT the same class the roadmap anticipated)

K_tr direction of the X mass is itself conditional input in the non-SUSY card: the SUSY theorem POSTULATED K_tr^-1(X,X); here the conjugate-class instanton must be textured along K_tr^-1 for the Schur step to reproduce lambda^2 K_tr (recorded in the price card)
portability: DYN-5's EXACT light-sector silence (seesaw invariance under any invertible redefinition of nu^c) is pure linear algebra and carries over to the non-SUSY card unchanged
price card assembled, with the honest 'none' for low-energy beyond-window consequences (pricing discipline allows an explicit none)

S1_no_go.consequence: charge bookkeeping alone can NEVER close the non-SUSY protection card; the separator must be non-charge data (instanton zero-mode structure)
S1_no_go.identity: q(XLH) - q(XX) = (q_X + q_L + q_H) - 2 q_X = -(q_X - q_L - q_H) = -(q_X + q_N) + (q_N + q_L + q_H) = 0 whenever the portal and the Dirac Yukawa are charge-allowed
S1_no_go.scope: any additive grading: U(1)^k, Z_N^k, gauged or global, anomalous or not, B-L included

S2_lorentz.note: the SUSY decoration danger audited in DYN-5 is a superpotential (scalar-component) artifact; in components it dissolves, and the whole burden lands on X L Htilde

S4_price_card.axiom_statement: an anomalous U(1) (GS-cancelled, Stueckelberg-massive; B-L-like) makes the messenger charge bookkeeping perturbatively EXACT, with all violations instanton-sourced AND the XX-mass instanton's zero modes saturating only the X bilinear (selectivity), textured along K_tr^-1
S4_price_card.beyond_window_consequence: NONE at low energy (X sits at sqrt(3) M_* ~ 6.8e15 GeV); the card's testable content is internal -- zero-mode counting on the two cycles in any concrete embedding
buys: perturbatively exact selection rules without holomorphy (gauge symmetry, all loop orders)
with selectivity: X-Dirac contamination pushed below the DYN-4 windows
cannot_buy: charge separation of XX from X L Htilde (S1 no-go: impossible for ANY abelian bookkeeping)
the K_tr texture of the X mass (new conditional input, as in D3)

closure_conditions.both_instanton_orientations: NN (+2) and XX (-2) classes with independent actions
